##NAMES=Wernerbaurite
##RRUFFID=R250081
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY={(NH_4_)_2_[Ca_2_(H_2_O)_14_](H_2_O)_2_}{V_10_O_28_}
##LOCALITY=St. Jude mine, Slick Rock district, San Miguel County, Colorado, USA
##OWNER=Natural History Museum of Los Angeles County
##SOURCE=Tony Kampf
##DESCRIPTION=Yellow-orange tabular crystals with stepped faces and square to octagonal outlines. Type sample, described in Kampf et al (2013)
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral is confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R250081
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY={(Ca_1.88_Na_0.14_K_0.03_Sr_0.02_)(H_2_O)_15.95_(H_3_O)_2.05_}(V_10_O_28_) ; Based on V^5+^ = 10 and O = 46
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Single crystal
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 9.7212 b: 10.2598 c: 10.5928 alpha: 89.999 beta: 77.083 gamma: 69.887 volume: 963.55 crystal system: triclinic
##FILETYPE=Raman RAW
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=532
